title: Access denied for custom certificate path in n8n Docker
symptom: When starting n8n with a custom SSL certificate path, an access denied error occurs. The logs indicate that n8n cannot access the specified directory for certificates.
applies_when: Using n8n in a Docker setup
Custom SSL certificate path is configured
root_cause: The error occurs because the n8n process does not have the necessary permissions to access the specified directory for custom certificates. This can happen if the directory permissions are not set correctly or if the user running the n8n process lacks access rights.
fix_steps: Check the permissions of the /opt/custom-certificates directory
Ensure that the directory is readable by the user running the n8n container
If necessary, change the permissions using 'chmod' or adjust the ownership using 'chown'.
agent_instruction: Ensure the n8n process has read access to the /opt/custom-certificates directory.
